_comment: Curated quarterly history for MHCLG delivery indicators. Every point cites its MHCLG statistical release (URL + publication date) and the raw upstream figure used. Values are derived, not fabricated — the derivations are defined in the 'methodology' block below.

housing_trajectory.rationale: Quarterly new-build dwelling completions (seasonally adjusted) annualised by x4, expressed as a percentage of the 300,000 homes-per-year government target that OBR uses as its housing trajectory working assumption. Deliberately uses completions (not annual 'net additional dwellings') so every quarter has a primary-source number; NAD is published once a year and is not a quarterly series.
housing_trajectory.primary_source: MHCLG Housing supply: indicators of new supply quarterly releases (headline 'Completions (seasonally adjusted)' figure)

planning_consents.rationale: Total residential decisions granted in the quarter, expressed as a percentage of an estimated 2019 pre-COVID quarterly baseline of 11,500. The 11,500 figure reflects the approximate 2019 quarterly mean for total residential decisions granted as documented in contemporary MHCLG planning-applications releases for that year. Later releases report only YoY percentage changes rather than primary Q-level figures for pre-2022 data, so we use an estimate derived from MHCLG narrative back-projections; the denominator can be tightened in a later revision once archived PDFs are manually extracted.
planning_consents.primary_source: MHCLG Planning applications in England quarterly statistical releases (headline 'residential decisions granted' figure)

baseline_note: Observations span 2022-Q2 through 2025-Q4 — the era in which MHCLG publishes the headline figures on HTML landing pages. Earlier releases are PDF-only and were not extracted in this iteration. ECDF normalisation against ~15 quarterly points gives a coarse but meaningful percentile ranking; more depth can be added by manually extracting older PDFs.

planning_consents._note: Components: 900 major + 6,300 minor = 7,200. The release landing page's rounded summary reported 7,300; the major+minor breakdown from the statistical release body sums to 7,200, which is authoritative.
